messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
Some people say the Symposium was written by Socrates — is that correct?

That is not correct. The Symposium (會飲篇) was written by Plato, not Socrates. As with the Republic, Socrates is a prominent character within the Symposium — he delivers one of the dialogue's most celebrated speeches on the nature of love and beauty, attributed to the priestess Diotima — but Socrates wrote nothing himself. All of the Socratic dialogues are Plato's compositions. Attributing the Symposium to Socrates misunderstands the relationship between these two figures.
